channel.title: Policy & Security Think Tank Updates
channel.description: Custom RSS feed from CSIS, CFR, and other policy institutions

item.title: Beyond Artemis: Why the Moon Matters for the Future of Space Operations
item.description: Returning to the Moon is not just about exploration. It is about extending space operations beyond Earth orbit, developing the capabilities, norms, and infrastructure needed to sustain activity in cislunar space, and shaping the future of how we operate in space.

item.title: Why Go Back to the Moon?
item.description: As the United States prepares to return humans to the Moon, the case rests on three strategic drivers: its role as a gateway to deep space, its valuable natural resources, and the growing risk of ceding leadership in space to China.

item.title: Artemis and the Economics of a Permanent Moonbase
item.description: Apollo proved we could go. Artemis must prove we can stay. That will require not just new technology, but a new approach to cost, partnerships, and political durability.

item.title: Cracking the Code on the Lunar Economy
item.description: To build a sustainable lunar economy, the United States must move beyond government-led models and adopt a commercially driven approach, treating the Moon as a real estate project anchored by public-private partnerships and an international Lunar Development Authority.

item.title: We Are Going Back to the Moon to Stay
item.description: NASA's plan to establish a base on the Moon is part of a strategic competition with China. The United States needs to be in it for the long game.
